originalTitle: ‘I took a deep breath’: Oliver Glasner emotional after perfect Crystal Palace send-off
summary: Oliver Glasner paid tribute to Crystal Palace’s players as he signed off as manager and insisted he has no regrets about his decision to depart
content: Oliver Glasner paid tribute to Crystal Palace’s players as he signed off as manager by winning the Conference League and insisted he has no regrets about his decision to depart despite making history in south London. Jean-Philippe Mateta’s goal in the second half means Glasner has now won three trophies in the space of 12 months after last year’s triumphs in the FA Cup and Community Shield. There were wild celebrations in Leipzig as the Austrian performed a full length slide on the pitch, while thousands of supporters who were watching the game on a big screen at Selhurst Park invaded the pitch. “I took a deep breath with the final whistle,” admitted an emotional Glasner, who said he was no “David Copperfield” when he was appointed to replace Roy Hodgson in February 2024. “I just supported this group of players – I could be the best magician but without the players it wouldn’t work, The players deserve all the credit because they have to trust me and work very hard. They will all tell you I’m very demanding. But they know I want the best for everyone. As soon as everyone understood that we created a fantastic spirit and the supporters now have what they deserve with a spot in the Europa League.” But asked whether he may reconsider his decisions to depart, he added: “No. I remember you asked me once, it was at the academy, when the fans prepared the banner, when we were struggling a little bit. And now it’s the same. I see myself as a servant for the players, as a servant for the club and then somebody who gives the direction. I told the players today in the meeting when we were in the hotel, because I got many messages from fans where they said: ‘Thank you for the best day of my life,’ and I really appreciate it. And I told the players exactly the same. So I have to say thank you to the players, I have to say thank you to the supporters, because it’s the same.” Adam Wharton was named man of the match despite admitting he had been a major doubt to start against Rayo Vallecano after limping off against Arsenal on Sunday. “I’ve pretty much had my foot in an icebox for the last couple of days trying to get the swelling down, I couldn’t shoot properly. It wasn’t comfortable but I wasn’t going to miss the final for a little swollen ankle,” he said. The Rayo manager, Iñigo Pérez, said he would find it hard to watch the match again after his side were denied a first major trophy in their history. “What breaks you inside is seeing the people cry, trying to cheer you up. They were superior in tactics and we were never close to winning,” he said.
chineseTitle: “我深吸了一口气”：奥利弗·格拉斯纳在完美告别水晶宫后情绪激动
chineseSummary: 奥利弗·格拉斯纳在辞去主教练职务时向水晶宫球员致敬，并坚称他对离开的决定并不后悔
chineseContent: 奥利弗·格拉斯纳(Oliver Glasner)在赢得会议联赛冠军后辞去水晶宫主帅职务时向水晶宫球员致敬，并坚称尽管在伦敦南部创造了历史，但他对离开的决定并不后悔。让·菲利普·马特塔下半场的进球意味着格拉斯纳继去年赢得足总杯和社区盾杯之后，在 12 个月的时间内赢得了三座奖杯。当这位奥地利人在球场上表演完整的滑行时，莱比锡爆发了疯狂的庆祝活动，而在塞尔赫斯特公园的大屏幕上观看比赛的数千名球迷则涌入了球场。 “终场哨声响起时，我深吸了一口气。”情绪激动的格拉斯纳承认道，他在 2024 年 2 月被任命接替罗伊·霍奇森时表示，自己不是“大卫·科波菲尔”。“我只是支持这群球员——我可以成为最好的魔术师，但如果没有球员，一切都行不通。球员们应该得到所有的荣誉，因为他们必须信任我并非常努力。他们都会告诉你我要求很高。但他们知道我希望每个人都能得到最好的。尽快每个人都明白，我们创造了一种梦幻般的精神，球迷们现在拥有了他们应得的欧罗巴联赛席位。”但当被问及是否会重新考虑离开的决定时，他补充道：“不。我记得你曾经问过我一次，那是在青训学院，当时球迷们准备了横幅，当时我们有点挣扎。现在也是一样。我把自己视为球员的仆人，俱乐部的仆人，然后是一个指明方向的人。今天我们在酒店的会议上，我告诉了球员们，因为我收到了很多球迷的留言，他们说：‘谢谢你们，让我度过了最美好的一天。’生活，”我真的很感激，所以我不得不说。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
